author: Augustine of Hippo
book: On the Morals of the Catholic Church.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Manichæan Controversy.
On the Morals of the Catholic Church.

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

title: Praise of the Clergy.

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.book: On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
verses.text: There is not, however, such narrowness in the moral excellence of the Catholic Church as that I should limit my praise of it to the life of those here mentioned.

verses.book: On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
verses.text: For how many bishops have I known most excellent and holy men, how many presbyters, how many deacons, and ministers of all kinds of the divine sacraments, whose virtue seems to me more admirable and more worthy of commendation on account of the greater difficulty of preserving it amidst the manifold varieties of men, and in this life of turmoil!

verses.book: On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
verses.text: For they preside over men needing cure as much as over those already cured.

verses.book: On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
verses.text: The vices of the crowd must be borne with in order that they may be cured, and the plague must be endured before it is subdued.

verses.book: On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
verses.text: To keep here the best way of life and a mind calm and peaceful is very hard.

verses.book: On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
verses.text: Here, in a word, we are among people who are learning to live.

verses.book: On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
